summary:
Both the coronoid and condylar processes develop from Meckel’s cartilage; thus, these portions of the mandible first ossify after forming as cartilaginous structures. All specified mandibular segments derive their origins from this common embryonic source, underscoring a shared pathway through early cartilage formation preceding bone growth. $ Agent1's reasoning summarized: 

Both the coronoid and condylar processes start as cartilage before turning into bone, making them examples of how some parts of the mandible undergo an initial phase where they are soft tissue rather than hard bone. This applies broadly since other named parts also follow similar developmental pathways involving Meckel’s cartilage. $\]
